summary: Preserve secondary multiple values when a non-local exit unwinds through an unwind-protect (fix dropped values in cl_pending_throw path).

items.title: Carry multiple values through pending throw across unwind-protect
items.description:
Root cause: when a non-local exit (return-from, throw, go, invoke-restart) detects an interposing UWPROT frame, the four call sites stash only a single primary value into cl_pending_value before longjmping to the UWPROT. The unwind-protect cleanup forms then run and clobber the global cl_mv_count / cl_mv_values. When OP_UWRETHROW re-throws to the actual catch/block target, it writes only nlx->result and never touches nlx->mv_count / nlx->mv_values, so OP_CATCH and OP_BLOCK_PUSH restore stale mv state (mv_count = 1, initialized at frame push) — secondaries are silently dropped. Repro: (defun cwlh (fn) (unwind-protect (funcall fn) nil)) (defun recv () (block done (cwlh (lambda () (return-from done (values nil t)))))) (multiple-value-list (recv)) => (NIL) instead of (NIL T).

Fix:

1. src/core/thread.h: extend the per-thread pending-throw state with mv preservation. In `struct CL_Thread`, just after the existing `pending_error_msg[512]` field (line 68), add:
   - `int pending_mv_count;`
   - `CL_Obj pending_mv_values[CL_MAX_MV];`
   And add the matching cl_pending_mv_count / cl_pending_mv_values macros in the CL_THREAD_NO_MACROS-guarded section (next to the existing cl_pending_* macros at lines 418-423).

2. src/core/vm.c — at every site that sets cl_pending_throw = 1 to longjmp into a UWPROT, also snapshot the current multiple-value state into the new pending_mv_* fields BEFORE the longjmp (so cleanup forms can't clobber it). Sites to patch:
   - OP_BLOCK_RETURN (line ~2722) — interposing-UWPROT branch
   - OP_TAGBODY_GO (line ~2837) — interposing-UWPROT branch (uniformity; OP_TAGBODY_PUSH restoration still forces cl_mv_count = 1, so this is just defensive copying)
   Pattern at each site, immediately after `cl_pending_value = ...;`:
     cl_pending_mv_count = cl_mv_count;
     { int mi; for (mi = 0; mi < cl_mv_count && mi < CL_MAX_MV; mi++)
         cl_pending_mv_values[mi] = cl_mv_values[mi]; }

3. src/core/vm.c OP_UWRETHROW (line ~3396) — when re-throwing pending_throw == 1 and the target is found with no further interposing UWPROT (the `cl_nlx_stack[i].result = pval;` branch around line 3421), also restore mv into the target NLX frame BEFORE the longjmp, so OP_CATCH / OP_BLOCK_PUSH restoration code picks them up. Add right after `cl_nlx_stack[i].result = pval;`:
     cl_nlx_stack[i].mv_count = cl_pending_mv_count;
     { int mi; for (mi = 0; mi < cl_pending_mv_count && mi < CL_MAX_MV; mi++)
         cl_nlx_stack[i].mv_values[mi] = cl_pending_mv_values[mi]; }
   (For TAGBODY targets these fields are unused by OP_TAGBODY_PUSH's restoration — it explicitly sets cl_mv_count = 1 — so this is safely uniform.)
   For the interposing-UWPROT branch within OP_UWRETHROW (line ~3416, where we longjmp to a deeper UWPROT), do NOT clear pending_mv_* — they must survive the next cleanup the same way pending_tag / pending_value already do. No change needed there.

4. src/core/builtins_condition.c cl_throw_to_tag (line ~1172) — interposing-UWPROT branch: same snapshot pattern as in vm.c (write pending_mv_count + pending_mv_values immediately after cl_pending_value = value).

5. src/core/builtins_io.c bi_throw (line ~1886) — interposing-UWPROT branch: same snapshot pattern as in vm.c.

No FASL version bump required: the change only affects in-memory VM state, not serialized bytecode or on-disk wire format.

Regression tests (host):
Add a new TEST in tests/test_vm.c near the existing unwind-protect multiple-value test (the eval_special_uwprot_mv style test around line 2425). Cases to cover:
  a. Exact request repro: (cwlh / recv / (multiple-value-list (recv))) -> (NIL T)
  b. More than 2 secondaries: (block b (unwind-protect (return-from b (values 1 2 3 4)) nil)) wrapped in multiple-value-list -> (1 2 3 4)
  c. Nested unwind-protects: (block b (unwind-protect (unwind-protect (return-from b (values 1 2 3)) (setq *log* (cons 'inner *log*))) (setq *log* (cons 'outer *log*)))) — multiple-value-list -> (1 2 3) and *log* contains both inner and outer markers in correct order.
  d. THROW across UWPROT carrying values: (catch 'k (unwind-protect (throw 'k (values 10 20)) nil)) wrapped in multiple-value-list -> (10 20)
  e. Cleanup forms producing their own (values ...) do not bleed: (block b (unwind-protect (return-from b (values 'a 'b)) (values 99 98 97))) -> (A B)
  f. (values) producing zero values through UWPROT: (block b (unwind-protect (return-from b (values)) nil)) multiple-value-list -> ()
  g. Restart-driven exit through UWPROT (uses cl_throw_to_tag path) — e.g. invoke-restart inside a handler-case across an unwind-protect; assert all values propagate.

Regression tests (Amiga):
Add a parallel suite to tests/amiga/run-tests.lisp covering cases (a), (c), (d) at minimum — these exercise the three throw-into-UWPROT call sites.

Verification:
  - make test  (host suite must stay green; new tests must pass)
  - make -f Makefile.cross test-amiga  (Amiga suite must stay green; new tests must pass)
  - Manual repro: ./build/host/clamiga --eval "(progn (defun cwlh (fn) (unwind-protect (funcall fn) nil)) (defun recv () (block done (cwlh (lambda () (return-from done (values nil t)))))) (print (multiple-value-list (recv))))" should print (NIL T).
